title: Getting Started with the OpenAI API: A Developer's Guide
description: Everything you need to know to start building with GPT-4o, embeddings, and the Assistants API. Includes working code examples.
author: MyAppAI Team
body:
The OpenAI API is the fastest way to add AI capabilities to any application. Whether you want to build a chatbot, summarize documents, generate code, or analyze images, the API gives you access to GPT-4o and other state-of-the-art models with a few lines of code.

Here's everything you need to get started.

## 1. Get Your API Key

Sign up at platform.openai.com and navigate to API Keys. Create a new secret key and store it somewhere safe — you won't be able to see it again.

Set it as an environment variable:

```bash
export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
```

## 2. Install the SDK

```bash
npm install openai
```

## 3. Your First API Call

```javascript
import OpenAI from 'openai'

const client = new OpenAI()

const response = await client.chat.completions.create({
  model: 'gpt-4o',
  messages: [
    { role: 'user', content: 'Explain how React hooks work in 3 sentences.' }
  ]
})

console.log(response.choices[0].message.content)
```

## 4. Streaming Responses

For a better user experience, stream the response as it generates:

```javascript
const stream = await client.chat.completions.create({
  model: 'gpt-4o',
  messages: [{ role: 'user', content: 'Write a short poem about coding.' }],
  stream: true
})

for await (const chunk of stream) {
  process.stdout.write(chunk.choices[0]?.delta?.content || '')
}
```

## 5. System Prompts

System prompts let you define the AI's persona and behavior:

```javascript
const response = await client.chat.completions.create({
  model: 'gpt-4o',
  messages: [
    {
      role: 'system',
      content: 'You are a senior software engineer. Give concise, practical answers. Always include code examples.'
    },
    {
      role: 'user',
      content: 'How do I debounce a function in JavaScript?'
    }
  ]
})
```

## 6. Vision — Analyzing Images

GPT-4o can analyze images directly:

```javascript
const response = await client.chat.completions.create({
  model: 'gpt-4o',
  messages: [{
    role: 'user',
    content: [
      { type: 'text', text: 'What is in this image?' },
      { type: 'image_url', image_url: { url: 'https://example.com/image.jpg' } }
    ]
  }]
})
```

## 7. Embeddings

Embeddings convert text into vectors for semantic search and similarity:

```javascript
const embedding = await client.embeddings.create({
  model: 'text-embedding-3-small',
  input: 'The quick brown fox'
})

console.log(embedding.data[0].embedding) // Array of 1536 numbers
```

## Pricing Tips

- GPT-4o mini is 15x cheaper than GPT-4o and handles most tasks well
- Cache your responses when possible — repeated identical prompts cost the same
- Use `max_tokens` to cap response length and control costs
- Monitor usage at platform.openai.com/usage

## What to Build Next

Now that you have the basics, here are some ideas:

- **Document Q&A:** Upload PDFs and let users ask questions
- **Code review bot:** Analyze PRs and suggest improvements
- **Content generator:** Automate blog posts, product descriptions, or social copy
- **Customer support:** Build a chatbot trained on your documentation

The OpenAI API is the foundation of most AI products being built today. Once you understand the basics, the possibilities are nearly unlimited.
